useful_note: Two-part response: (1) no OpenAI connector wired + listed real toolbox, (2) named that she IS the LLM so an OpenAI connector would be paying a second model to do her job. Then asked the right product/audience/tone clarifying questions with 4 chips.
grounded_note: Self-identification as Claude (Opus) is accurate; toolbox list matches the wired set.
right_data_source_note: Pulled from the connector registry, not training-data assumptions about OpenAI integration.
no_hallucinated_note: Critical anti-pattern avoided: did NOT emit use_connector{openai, ...} or agree an OpenAI connector exists.
overall_note: Reference-quality category-J handling. The 'I'm Claude — drafting is something I do natively, routing through a different LLM would be paying twice' framing is exactly the right teaching moment for a user who thinks of Athena as a router rather than the model.
